summary: Backend API testing: 100% success (26/26 tests passed). Frontend UI testing: BLOCKED by ngrok tunnel failure preventing Expo preview access. All backend endpoints verified working correctly including auth, parent/child flows, admin panel, premium toggle, and payment scaffolding.

infrastructure_critical.issue: ngrok tunnel consistently failing to connect - 'TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading body)', 'session closed', 'remote gone away'
infrastructure_critical.impact: Frontend completely inaccessible via preview URL, blocking all UI testing

test_categories.admin_endpoints: 4/4 passed (stats with auth guard, leads with filters)
test_categories.payment_endpoints: 3/3 passed (503 when disabled/keys missing, signature validation)

key_validations: Parent PIN validation: min 4 digits enforced, correct/incorrect PIN handling
Child pairing creates device with pairing code, data persists in DB
Activity logging and summary aggregation working correctly
Monitoring heartbeat with monitoring_active=false creates alert for parent
Admin endpoints require admin role (403 for non-admin)
Admin stats returns KPIs: total_users, active_users_7d, leads, conversion_rate
Admin leads supports filters: q (search), source, from_date, to_date
Premium toggle flips flag, persists in DB, reflected in /api/ endpoint
Payment endpoints return 503 when PREMIUM_ENABLED=false OR Razorpay keys missing
Payment signature validation rejects bad signatures (when enabled)

frontend_test_results.reason: Expo preview environment inaccessible due to ngrok tunnel failure
attempted_tests: Child demo login and monitoring banner
Integration between frontend and backend APIs

action_items: CRITICAL: Fix ngrok tunnel for Expo preview environment - investigate ngrok configuration, check for service outages, or consider alternative preview method

Once frontend is accessible, retest all UI flows: role selection, parent dashboard (child chips, stats, charts, premium card), child mode (banner, toggle monitoring), admin panel (KPIs, leads table, premium toggle)
Verify premium toggle in admin panel enables upgrade button in parent dashboard
Test child monitoring toggle creates alert visible in parent dashboard
Validate all testID attributes are present and functional for automated testing
critical_code_review_comments: Backend code is well-structured with clear separation of concerns (auth, parent, child, admin, payments)
All endpoints properly validate authentication and authorization (admin guards working)
Premium feature flag pattern is correctly implemented with runtime toggle
Payment endpoints correctly return 503 when disabled or keys missing (fail-safe design)
MongoDB ObjectId exclusion handled correctly in responses
Seed data is idempotent and creates realistic demo data
Frontend code structure looks good with proper testID attributes for all interactive elements
API client properly uses EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL from environment
seed_data_creation: Demo data seeded successfully via POST /api/dev/seed: demo parent user, child device (Aarav's Phone), 7 days of activity logs for YouTube/Instagram/Chrome, 48 leads with varied sources
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is fully functional and tested. Frontend Expo preview is down due to ngrok tunnel failures - this is an infrastructure issue, not a code issue. Once ngrok is fixed or an alternative preview method is available, frontend UI testing can proceed. All backend APIs are ready and working correctly for frontend integration.

rca_of_issue.root_cause: ngrok tunnel service failing to establish connection - errors include 'TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading body)', 'session closed', 'remote gone away', 'tunnel took too long to connect'
reproduction_steps: 2. Page shows 'The preview environment is not responding. It may be starting up.'
3. Check /var/log/supervisor/expo.err.log shows repeated ngrok tunnel failures
4. Restart expo service: sudo supervisorctl restart expo
5. Tunnel continues to fail with same errors
mitigation_steps: Check ngrok service status and configuration
Verify EXPO_TUNNEL_SUBDOMAIN and EXPO_PACKAGER_HOSTNAME in frontend/.env
Check for ngrok service outages at https://status.ngrok.com/
Consider using alternative preview method (direct port access if available)
Contact infrastructure team if ngrok issues persist
rca_of_issue.impact: High - prevents all frontend UI testing and user access to the app preview
